title: Deep integration: 9 services wired into 3 user-facing endpoints + TA signal in composite + 10 frontend API functions

bugs_found: test_stock_signal_components expected 11 component keys, needed update after adding 'technical' component
observation.gap_identified: Many standalone services (VIX term structure, economic surprise, sector rotation, trends attention, drawdown analyzer, conformal predictor, factor model, technical analysis) were not wired into the main user-facing endpoints (market-status, stock analysis, portfolio analyze). Users had to call separate endpoints to get this data.

bugs_fixed: Updated test_stock_signal_components to expect 12 component keys (added 'technical')
implementation.feature_built: 9 services integrated into 3 main endpoints: (1) market-status now includes VIX term structure, economic surprise index, sector rotation; (2) stock analysis now includes trends attention, drawdown analysis, conformal crash intervals; (3) portfolio analyze now includes FF5 factor exposures. TA signal (RSI/MACD/BB/ADX) added as 13th component in stock signal engine. 10 missing frontend API functions added.

limitations: Drawdown analysis in stock endpoint fetches 5y data via yfinance which adds latency
Google Trends (pytrends) can be rate-limited, trends_attention may timeout in stock analysis
Factor exposures in portfolio analyze make an additional data fetch (FF data from Kenneth French Library)
TA signal in screener adds ~1 yfinance call per stock which increases screener latency
Sector rotation in market-status fetches sector ETFs which adds latency to an already heavy endpoint
assessment.self_critique: Could have added caching to the new integrations (especially drawdown and TA in screener) to mitigate latency. The TA signal in the screener duplicates the yfinance fetch that analyze_stock already does internally — ideally the TA should be computed inside analyze_stock and returned as part of its result dict to avoid the extra fetch. Frontend types for new market-status fields could be more specific.

next_steps: Cache drawdown_analysis and trends_attention results with TTL to reduce per-stock latency
Move TA computation into analyze_stock() to avoid duplicate yfinance fetches in screener
Build frontend UI components to display new market-status fields (VIX term structure widget, economic surprise gauge, sector rotation heatmap)
Add survival model endpoint (Cox PH) — currently has no router
Wire conformal intervals into the screener's per-stock crash_prob display
Consider lazy-loading non-critical integrations (trends, drawdown) in stock analysis to improve response time
